entities.name: Deduplication Script
entities.context: Python script that identifies and removes duplicate observations and workouts from a database, with dry-run safety built in

entities.name: Dry Run Pattern
entities.context: Script implements --execute flag; defaults to dry-run mode, aligning with V's P35-P39 building principles requiring --dry-run in every script

entities.name: apply_plan
entities.context: Function that executes the deletion plan against a database connection, returning counts of deleted observations and workouts

entities.name: Observations
entities.context: Data entity being deduplicated; each group has a canonical_obs_id to keep and duplicate_obs_ids to delete

entities.name: Workouts
entities.context: Data entity linked to observations; each group has a canonical_workout_id to keep and workouts_to_delete to remove

entities.name: external_id
entities.context: Grouping key used to identify duplicate records — records sharing an external_id are candidates for deduplication

relationships.context: Script defaults to dry-run and requires explicit --execute flag before applying destructive changes
Main function calls apply_plan only when --execute is passed
Observations and workouts are paired; deduplication preserves canonical pairs and deletes duplicates together
Duplicate observations are grouped by shared external_id
Script manages lifecycle of observation records — keeps canonical, deletes duplicates
Script manages lifecycle of workout records — keeps canonical, deletes duplicates
